rationale: LUNC's net supply dynamics are governed by two competing forces: (1) a community-passed 1.2% on-chain tax burn (partially implemented, currently ~0.2% effective burn on on-chain volume) and (2) staking rewards emitted from the community pool and validator commissions. As of mid-2025, CoinGecko reports a circulating supply of approximately 5.71–5.73 trillion LUNC, but precise 12-month supply-change figures require historical snapshot data not freely exportable from CoinGecko's public UI without API pagination. The Terra Classic governance forum and Agora (agora.terra.money) indicate the chain is in a net-deflationary or near-zero-inflation regime on paper due to burn mechanics, but actual realized inflation/deflation depends on transaction volume driving burns versus staking emission rates — a precise annualized figure cannot be reliably computed from freely available snapshots alone.
